qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended method for users to install their SSH public key so it is available to all clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Users are encouraged to leverage the new CCDB feature to install their SSH public key. This involves grabbing the content of your public key (e.g., 'id_rsa.pub') and uploading it to CCDB as per the provided instructions.

qa_pairs.question: What is considered the simplest and safest command to install an SSH public key on a remote system?
qa_pairs.answer: The simplest and safest way to install an SSH public key to a remote system is by using the `ssh-copy-id` command. An example usage is `ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/mynewkey.pub graham.computecanada.ca`, assuming the new keypair is named 'mynewkey' and your username on the remote machine is the same as your local username.
